title: Hydroponics system
abstract: A hydroponics system, which includes at least one upright planter assembly comprising a plurality of vertically spaced apart plant holders and at least one vertically extending liquid channel arranged along the plant holders to direct liquid flowing from top to bottom onto a root portion of plants to be held in the plant holders; a liquid receptacle at a bottom of the at least one planter assembly for collecting liquid at the bottom of the planter assembly and a pump arrangement for pumping liquid from the liquid receptacle to the top of the at least one upright planter assembly.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A hydroponics system, which includes at least one upright planter assembly comprising a plurality of vertically spaced apart plant holders and at least one vertically extending liquid channel arranged along the plant holders to direct liquid flowing from top to bottom onto a root portion of plants to be held in the plant holders; a liquid receptacle at a bottom of the at least one planter assembly for collecting liquid at the bottom of the planter assembly; and a pump arrangement for pumping liquid from the liquid receptacle to the top of the at least one upright planter assembly. 2. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 1 , which includes a lighting arrangement installed proximate the at least one upright planter assembly, the lighting arrangement in use directing light onto plants to be held in the plant holders. 3. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 2, in which the lighting arrangement is installed at a top end of the at least one upright planter assembly to direct light downward onto plants to be planted in the plant holders. 4. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 1 , which includes an aeration system having a vertically extending air channel arranged along the plant holders to direct air flowing from bottom to top onto a leaf portion of plants to be held in the plant holders. 5. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 1 , in which the at least one upright planter assembly comprises a plurality of planters vertically stacked onto each other, each planter having a longitudinal body defining a longitudinal channel which is internally divided into at least two longitudinally extending liquid channels; at least two plant holders defined in the body, the plant holders spaced on the outer circumference of the longitudinal body, each one of the at least two plant holders opening into one of the at least two longitudinally extending liquid channels. 6. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 5, in which the planters include connecting formations at the ends of the longitudinal body. 7. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 6, in which the connecting formations is in the form of socket and spigot formations shaped and dimensioned to fit into respective spigot and socket formations of corresponding planters. 8. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 7, in which the operative lower end of the longitudinal body of the planter has a spigot formation, for fitting into a socket formation on an operative upper end of a longitudinal body of a corresponding planter installed below the former planter. 9. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 5, in which each plant holder includes an inlet opening and an outlet opening, which is in flow communication with its corresponding liquid channel. 10. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 9, in which the inlet opening is located above the outlet opening to permit gravitational liquid flow into the plant holder through the inlet opening and liquid flow from the plant holder through the outlet opening. 11. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 6, in which the planter includes at least one longitudinally extending air channel, the air channel having air outlets spaced along its length. 12. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 11 , in which the air outlets open onto any one of the outside of the planter body and into the inside of the at least two longitudinally extending liquid channels. 13. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 1 1 , in which the air channels include sidewardly directed air outlets. 14. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 11 , in which the connecting formations are shaped and dimensioned to connect adjacent planter bodies together. 15. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 14, in which the connecting formations include liquid channel connecting formations for connecting the liquid channels of adjacent planter bodies together. 16. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 14, in which the connecting formations include air channel connecting formations for connecting the air channels of adjacent planter bodies together. 17. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 5, in which the planter includes an external attachment formation proximate the plant holder mouth for attaching plant supports. 18. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 17, in which the attachment formation is located below the plant holder mouth for receiving various types of plant supports. 19. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 18, in which, the plant support is a creeper trellis and in which the creeper trellis is arranged to support a creeper plant, planted in the plant holder when it grows from the plant holder mouth. 20. A hydroponics system as claimed in claim 1 , substantially as herein described and illustrated. 21. A new hydroponics system, substantially as herein described.
